key_insight: A physics-informed neural network can learn transient elastodynamic wave propagation in bimaterial solids while enforcing elasticity, interface, and boundary physics directly in the loss.
engineering_relevance: This enables a fast surrogate for split Hopkinson pressure bar and other high-rate impact analyses, reducing dependence on repeated finite-element runs.
method: Axisymmetric linear elastodynamics embedded into a PINN loss
Steel-aluminum bimaterial specimen with initial, boundary, and interface conditions
ANSYS Explicit Dynamics used for validation and extra training constraints
evidence_signal: The model matches finite-element displacement, stress, strain, and wave transmission/reflection histories, and generalizes to unseen times and modified material properties.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of finite-element constraints and training data, especially near the bimaterial interface and under changed materials.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed Neural Network Framework for Elastodynamic Wave Propagation in Bimaterial Systems
